member.name: T:SimpleAuth.Client
member.summary: Registered OAuth 2.1 / OIDC client. All redirect URIs are validated by exact string match — no wildcards, no trailing-slash tolerance.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.ClientId
member.summary: Unique client identifier (client_id in RFC 6749 §2.2).

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.ClientName
member.summary: Human-readable display name shown in consent UI.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.Description
member.summary: Optional description shown in consent and admin UI.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.Enabled
member.summary: Whether this client is active. Disabled clients are rejected at every protocol endpoint.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.AllowedGrantTypes
member.summary: Grant types this client may request. Allowed values: , , .

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.RedirectUris
member.summary: Registered redirect URIs. The redirect_uri in an authorization request MUST exactly match one entry — RFC 9700 §2.1.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.PostLogoutRedirectUris
member.summary: Registered post-logout redirect URIs for end-session requests.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.AllowedCorsOrigins
member.summary: Origins allowed for CORS pre-flight on the token and revocation endpoints.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.TokenEndpointAuthMethod
member.summary: Token endpoint authentication method. Allowed values: , , , .

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.RequireClientSecret
member.summary: Whether this client must present a credential. Public clients set this to false.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.ClientCredentials
member.summary: Hashed credentials for this client. See .

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.Jwks
member.summary: Inline JWKS for private_key_jwt authentication. Mutually exclusive with a remote JWKS URI.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.JwksUri
member.summary: Remote JWKS URI for private_key_jwt. Fetched and cached at startup.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.RequirePkce
member.summary: Whether PKCE is required. Always true for public clients. Defaults to true for confidential clients as well per OAuth 2.1 best practice.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.AllowedScopes
member.summary: Scopes this client is allowed to request.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.AllowOfflineAccess
member.summary: Whether this client may request offline access (refresh tokens). Requires offline_access in and in .

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.AccessTokenLifetime
member.summary: Access token lifetime. Default: 1 hour.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.AuthorizationCodeLifetime
member.summary: Authorization code lifetime. Default: 5 minutes (RFC 6749 §4.1.2 recommends short).

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.RefreshTokenLifetime
member.summary: Refresh token absolute lifetime. Default: 30 days.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.IdentityTokenLifetime
member.summary: ID token lifetime. Must be short — the OIDC Core spec recommends minutes not hours. Default: 5 minutes.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.RefreshTokenUsage
member.summary: Refresh token usage strategy.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.RefreshTokenExpiration
member.summary: Refresh token expiration strategy.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.AccessTokenType
member.summary: Format of issued access tokens.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.Claims
member.summary: Static claims always included in tokens for this client.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.AlwaysIncludeUserClaimsInIdToken
member.summary: Whether to always include user claims in the ID token, even when a UserInfo endpoint call would suffice.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.RequireConsent
member.summary: Whether this client requires explicit user consent. Default: true.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.AllowRememberConsent
member.summary: Whether to remember consent across sessions for this client.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.SubjectType
member.summary: Subject identifier strategy. Default: public.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Client.PairwiseSectorIdentifierUri
member.summary: Sector identifier URI used to compute pairwise subjects (RFC OIDC Core §8.1). Required when is .

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.TokenUsage
member.summary: Controls whether a refresh token can be reused or is one-time-only.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.TokenUsage.ReUse
member.summary: The refresh token can be reused multiple times until expiry.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.TokenUsage.OneTimeOnly
member.summary: The refresh token is invalidated after a single use; a new one is issued.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.TokenExpiration
member.summary: Controls whether a token's lifetime extends on activity.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.TokenExpiration.Absolute
member.summary: Token expires at a fixed point regardless of activity.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.TokenExpiration.Sliding
member.summary: Token lifetime resets on each successful use.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.AccessTokenType
member.summary: Format of an issued access token.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.AccessTokenType.Jwt
member.summary: JWT access token — self-contained, verifiable without introspection.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.AccessTokenType.Reference
member.summary: Opaque reference token — requires introspection to validate.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.SubjectType
member.summary: Pairwise or public subject identifier strategy per OIDC Core §8.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.SubjectType.Public
member.summary: The same sub is returned to all clients.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.SubjectType.Pairwise
member.summary: A client-specific sub is derived from the sector identifier. Computed as BASE64URL(HMAC-SHA256(sector_identifier + public_sub, pairwise_salt)).

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.ClaimsSource
member.summary: Indicates which token type triggered a claims enrichment request.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.ClaimsSource.IdentityToken
member.summary: Claims are being added to an ID token.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.ClaimsSource.AccessToken
member.summary: Claims are being added to a JWT access token.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.ClaimsSource.UserInfo
member.summary: Claims are being returned from the UserInfo endpoint.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.ClaimsEnrichmentContext
member.summary: Context passed to each during token and UserInfo issuance.
member.remarks: Enrichers should only add identity claims (e.g., name, email). Protocol claims (sub, iss, aud, exp, iat, scope) are managed exclusively by the server and are ignored if added here.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ClaimsEnrichmentContext.SubjectId
member.summary: Subject (user) identifier — always present for user-bound tokens.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ClaimsEnrichmentContext.ClientId
member.summary: OAuth client identifier that originated the request.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ClaimsEnrichmentContext.GrantedScopes
member.summary: Scopes that were granted to this request.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ClaimsEnrichmentContext.Claims
member.summary: Claims produced by this enrichment run. Populate this collection to include additional claims in the ID token and UserInfo response.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.IClaimsEnricher
member.summary: Enriches identity claims for ID tokens and UserInfo responses.
member.remarks: Register one or more implementations via the standard DI container: services.AddScoped<IClaimsEnricher, MyProfileEnricher>(); All registered enrichers are invoked in registration order before token issuance. Enrichers are not called for machine-to-machine (client credentials) tokens.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IClaimsEnricher.EnrichAsync(SimpleAuth.ClaimsEnrichmentContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Adds identity claims to ..

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.IClientStore
member.summary: Read-only lookup of registered records.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IClientStore.FindByClientIdAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Finds a client by its . Returns if the client is not registered or is disabled.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.IResourceStore
member.summary: Read-only lookup of , and records.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IResourceStore.FindScopesByNameAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all enabled entries matching the given .

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IResourceStore.FindIdentityScopesByNameAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all enabled entries matching the given .

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IResourceStore.FindResourcesByScopeAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all enabled entries that contain at least one of the requested .

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IResourceStore.FindResourceByNameAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns a by its unique .

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IResourceStore.GetAllEnabledIdentityScopesAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all enabled identity scopes. Used to build the discovery document.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IResourceStore.GetAllEnabledScopesAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all enabled API scopes. Used to build the discovery document.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.ISigningKeyStore
member.summary: Persists and retrieves records for key rotation.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.ISigningKeyStore.GetActiveKeysAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all active signing keys (not yet retired or removed).

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.ISigningKeyStore.GetPrimaryKeyAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns the current primary signing key.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.ISigningKeyStore.AddAsync(SimpleAuth.SigningKeyInfo,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Adds a new signing key to the store.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.ISigningKeyStore.SetPrimaryAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sets the specified key as the primary signing key. Previous primary is demoted.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.ISigningKeyStore.RemoveExpiredAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Removes signing keys whose is in the past.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.IJtiStore
member.summary: Prevents replay of private_key_jwt assertions by tracking consumed JTIs. Implementations MUST be thread-safe and, in distributed deployments, use a shared store (e.g., Redis).

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IJtiStore.TryConsumeAsync(System.String,System.DateTime,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Atomically records a JTI and returns if it was new. Returns if the JTI was already present (replay detected). The JTI is automatically expired after .
member.remarks: Redis implementations should use SET key NX EX seconds. SQL implementations should use a unique constraint with a background cleanup job.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.IConsentStore
member.summary: Stores and retrieves records.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IConsentStore.FindAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns the stored consent for a subject/client pair, or if none exists. Expired consents are returned as .

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IConsentStore.StoreAsync(SimpleAuth.UserConsent,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Stores or updates a consent record.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IConsentStore.RemoveAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Removes the consent record for a subject/client pair.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.IAuthorizationCodeStore
member.summary: Stores and retrieves records. All operations on codes MUST be atomic to prevent replay attacks.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IAuthorizationCodeStore.StoreAsync(SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Persists a newly issued authorization code.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IAuthorizationCodeStore.ConsumeAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Atomically retrieves and marks a code as consumed. Returns if the code is not found, already consumed, or expired.
member.remarks: This operation MUST be atomic. Implementations using SQL should use a single UPDATE ... OUTPUT or equivalent. Implementations using Redis should use a Lua script. A second call with the same handle MUST return .

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IAuthorizationCodeStore.RemoveAllAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Removes all authorization codes for the given subject and client (e.g., on logout).

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.IRefreshTokenStore
member.summary: Stores and retrieves records.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IRefreshTokenStore.StoreAsync(SimpleAuth.RefreshToken,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Persists a newly issued refresh token.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IRefreshTokenStore.FindByHandleAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Retrieves a refresh token by its handle without consuming it. Returns if not found, expired, or revoked.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IRefreshTokenStore.ReplaceAsync(System.String,SimpleAuth.RefreshToken,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Replaces an existing refresh token with a new one (rotation). The old token MUST be atomically invalidated and the new token persisted.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IRefreshTokenStore.RevokeAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Revokes a specific refresh token.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IRefreshTokenStore.RevokeAllAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Revokes all refresh tokens for the given subject and client.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.IParStore
member.summary: Stores and retrieves records for Pushed Authorization Requests (RFC 9126).

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IParStore.StoreAsync(SimpleAuth.ParEntry,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Persists a newly received PAR entry.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.IParStore.ConsumeAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Atomically retrieves and removes a PAR entry by its . Returns if not found or expired. Each entry MUST be consumable only once.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.ITokenStore
member.summary: Stores and retrieves records for revocation and introspection.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.ITokenStore.StoreAsync(SimpleAuth.IssuedToken,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Persists a record of an issued access token.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.ITokenStore.FindByHandleAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Finds an issued token record by its reference handle. Returns if the token is not found, expired, or revoked.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.ITokenStore.RevokeAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Marks an issued token as revoked.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.ITokenStore.RevokeByRefreshTokenAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Revokes all access tokens associated with the given . Called when a refresh token is revoked.

member.name: M:SimpleAuth.ITokenStore.RevokeAllAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Revokes all access tokens for the given subject and client (e.g., on logout).

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.Scope
member.summary: An OAuth 2.0 scope as defined in RFC 6749 §3.3, associated with an API. Scopes are the unit of access clients request and resource servers validate.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Scope.Name
member.summary: Scope name as it appears in token requests (e.g., "api1", "read").

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Scope.DisplayName
member.summary: Human-readable display name for consent UI.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Scope.Description
member.summary: Human-readable description shown in consent UI.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Scope.ShowInDiscoveryDocument
member.summary: Whether this scope is shown in discovery. Default: true.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Scope.Required
member.summary: Whether this scope is required. If false, the user may opt out in consent.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Scope.Emphasize
member.summary: Whether to emphasize this scope on the consent page.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Scope.Enabled
member.summary: Whether this scope is enabled. Disabled scopes are rejected at authorization.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.IdentityScope
member.summary: An identity scope mapping an OIDC scope name to the set of claims it grants. Models the OIDC Core §5.4 standard claim scopes (profile, email, etc.) as well as custom identity scopes.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IdentityScope.Name
member.summary: Scope name (e.g., "openid", "profile", "email").

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IdentityScope.DisplayName
member.summary: Human-readable display name for consent UI.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IdentityScope.Description
member.summary: Human-readable description shown in consent UI.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IdentityScope.ClaimTypes
member.summary: Claim types granted when this scope is requested. The claims are provided by IClaimsEnricher implementations.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IdentityScope.Required
member.summary: Whether this scope is required; users cannot uncheck it in consent.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IdentityScope.Emphasize
member.summary: Whether to emphasize this scope on the consent page.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IdentityScope.ShowInDiscoveryDocument
member.summary: Whether this scope is shown in the discovery document.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IdentityScope.Enabled
member.summary: Whether this scope is enabled.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.ProtectedResource
member.summary: A protected resource as defined in RFC 8707 (Resource Indicators for OAuth 2.0). A resource groups one or more values and owns the credentials used by resource servers to call the introspection endpoint.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ProtectedResource.Name
member.summary: Unique resource name. Typically a URI matching the resource server audience.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ProtectedResource.DisplayName
member.summary: Human-readable display name.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ProtectedResource.Scopes
member.summary: Scope names that belong to this resource.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ProtectedResource.ApiCredentials
member.summary: Credentials used by the resource server when calling the introspection endpoint. See .

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ProtectedResource.Enabled
member.summary: Whether this resource is enabled.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.StandardScope
member.summary: Standard OIDC scope names per OIDC Core §5.4. Use these constants when registering default entries.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.StandardScope.OpenId
member.summary: The mandatory OpenID Connect scope. Triggers ID token issuance.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.StandardScope.Profile
member.summary: Profile claims: name, family_name, given_name, etc.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.StandardScope.Email
member.summary: Email claims: email, email_verified.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.StandardScope.Address
member.summary: Address claim: address.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.StandardScope.Phone
member.summary: Phone claims: phone_number, phone_number_verified.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.StandardScope.OfflineAccess
member.summary: Offline access — enables refresh token issuance.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.GrantType
member.summary: Standard OAuth 2.1 grant type names (RFC 6749 + OAuth 2.1 draft). Only three grant types exist in OAuth 2.1; implicit and ROPC are removed.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.GrantType.AuthorizationCode
member.summary: Authorization Code grant with mandatory PKCE (OAuth 2.1 §4.1).

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.GrantType.ClientCredentials
member.summary: Client Credentials grant for machine-to-machine flows (OAuth 2.1 §4.2).

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.GrantType.RefreshToken
member.summary: Refresh Token grant (OAuth 2.1 §4.3). Requires offline_access scope.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.AuthMethod
member.summary: Token endpoint authentication method names per OIDC Discovery §3.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.AuthMethod.ClientSecretBasic
member.summary: HTTP Basic authentication with client_id:client_secret (RFC 6749 §2.3.1).

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.AuthMethod.ClientSecretPost
member.summary: Credentials in the request body as form parameters.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.AuthMethod.PrivateKeyJwt
member.summary: Signed JWT assertion per RFC 7523.

member.name: F:SimpleAuth.AuthMethod.None
member.summary: No authentication — public clients only.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.Resources
member.summary: Pre-built standard registrations per OIDC Core §5.4.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.Resources.Standard
member.summary: The minimal set of standard identity scopes: openid, profile, email.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode
member.summary: An authorization code issued at the end of the authorize endpoint redirect. Codes are single-use — consuming the same code twice revokes all tokens in the grant (RFC 9700 §2.10).

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.Code
member.summary: Opaque handle sent to the client. SHA-256 hashed before storage.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.ClientId
member.summary: Client this code was issued to.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.SubjectId
member.summary: Subject (user) identifier.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.RedirectUri
member.summary: Redirect URI used in the original authorization request.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.CodeChallenge
member.summary: SHA-256 PKCE code challenge (RFC 7636 §4.2). null when client does not require PKCE.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.GrantedScopes
member.summary: Scopes granted at the authorization endpoint.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.CreatedAt
member.summary: UTC time this code was created.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.ExpiresAt
member.summary: UTC time this code expires. Typically 5 minutes from creation.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.Nonce
member.summary: Nonce from the authorization request, included in the ID token.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.AuthTime
member.summary: Unix timestamp (seconds) of when the End-User was authenticated. Populated when the authorization request included max_age. OIDC Core §3.1.2.1: auth_time MUST appear in the ID token when max_age was used.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.AcrValue
member.summary: Authentication Context Class Reference value to include in the ID token. Set from the first value of the acr_values request parameter (OIDC Core §3.1.2.1).

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.SessionId
member.summary: Session identifier from the user's authentication session.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.AuthorizationCode.IsConsumed
member.summary: Whether this code has been consumed. Used for replay detection.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken
member.summary: An opaque refresh token reference. Only reference tokens are supported — JWTs as refresh tokens are not issued. The is SHA-256 hashed before storage.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken.Handle
member.summary: Opaque handle. SHA-256 hashed in the store.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken.ClientId
member.summary: Client this refresh token was issued to.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken.SubjectId
member.summary: Subject (user) identifier.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken.GrantedScopes
member.summary: Scopes granted to this refresh token. Cannot exceed the original grant.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken.CreatedAt
member.summary: UTC creation time.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken.ExpiresAt
member.summary: UTC absolute expiry.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken.SlidingExpiresAt
member.summary: Sliding lifetime extension per refresh. Null when using absolute expiration.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken.SessionId
member.summary: Session identifier linked to this token family.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken.IsRevoked
member.summary: Whether this refresh token has been revoked.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken.Generation
member.summary: Generation counter for token family (used to detect replay within a family).

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.RefreshToken.DPopJkt
member.summary: JWK thumbprint (RFC 7638) of the DPoP public key bound to this refresh token. Non-null when the original grant used DPoP (RFC 9449 §10.1). On refresh, the server MUST require a DPoP proof with a matching thumbprint.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.IssuedToken
member.summary: A record of an issued access token, used for revocation and introspection. Stored by reference handle; the JWT itself is not persisted.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IssuedToken.Handle
member.summary: Opaque reference handle. SHA-256 hashed in the store.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IssuedToken.ClientId
member.summary: Client this token was issued to.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IssuedToken.SubjectId
member.summary: Subject identifier. Null for client credentials tokens.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IssuedToken.GrantedScopes
member.summary: Granted scopes in this token.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IssuedToken.CreatedAt
member.summary: UTC creation time.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IssuedToken.ExpiresAt
member.summary: UTC expiry time.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IssuedToken.IsRevoked
member.summary: Whether this token has been revoked.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IssuedToken.RefreshTokenHandle
member.summary: Handle of the refresh token this access token was issued alongside. Null if issued via client credentials or if no refresh token was issued.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.IssuedToken.JktThumbprint
member.summary: JWK thumbprint (RFC 7638) of the DPoP public key bound to this token. Non-null only when the token was issued with a DPoP header (RFC 9449). When set, resource endpoints MUST require a matching DPoP proof.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.UserConsent
member.summary: A recorded user consent decision for a specific client and scope set. Enables skipping the consent screen on repeated visits.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.UserConsent.SubjectId
member.summary: Subject (user) identifier.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.UserConsent.ClientId
member.summary: Client identifier.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.UserConsent.GrantedScopes
member.summary: Scopes the user approved.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.UserConsent.CreatedAt
member.summary: UTC time this consent was given.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.UserConsent.ExpiresAt
member.summary: UTC time this consent expires. Null means no expiry.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.ParEntry
member.summary: A pushed authorization request (RFC 9126). Stores the full authorize request parameters, keyed by a short-lived .

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.RequestUri
member.summary: Unique request URI in the form urn:ietf:params:oauth:request-uri:{handle}. Used as the lookup key; handle is not hashed (short-lived, no secret value).

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.ClientId
member.summary: Client that submitted this PAR request (already authenticated).

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.RedirectUri
member.summary: Redirect URI from the PAR request.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.Scope
member.summary: Space-separated scope string from the PAR request.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.CodeChallenge
member.summary: PKCE code challenge (S256 is the only supported method). Null when PKCE is not used.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.CodeChallengeMethod
member.summary: PKCE code challenge method — "S256" or null when PKCE is not used.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.ResponseType
member.summary: response_type from the PAR request. Should always be "code".

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.State
member.summary: Optional opaque state forwarded to the redirect URI.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.Nonce
member.summary: Optional nonce bound to the ID token.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.ResponseMode
member.summary: Optional response_mode (e.g., "query", "form_post").

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.CreatedAt
member.summary: UTC time this PAR entry was created.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ParEntry.ExpiresAt
member.summary: UTC time this PAR entry expires. RFC 9126 recommends ≤ 90 s.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.SigningKeyInfo
member.summary: Metadata for a signing key managed by ISigningKeyStore.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.SigningKeyInfo.KeyId
member.summary: Key identifier. Format: {alg}-{yyyyMMddHHmmss}-{4hex} (e.g., ec-20260120-a3f2).

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.SigningKeyInfo.Algorithm
member.summary: Signing algorithm (e.g., "ES256", "RS256").

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.SigningKeyInfo.PrivateKeyPem
member.summary: PEM-encoded private key. Stored encrypted at rest.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.SigningKeyInfo.CreatedAt
member.summary: UTC time this key was created.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.SigningKeyInfo.RetireAt
member.summary: UTC time after which this key should no longer sign new tokens.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.SigningKeyInfo.RemoveAt
member.summary: UTC time after which this key should be removed from JWKS.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.SigningKeyInfo.IsPrimary
member.summary: Whether this key is the current (primary) signing key.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.ClientCredential
member.summary: A hashed credential (secret, X.509 thumbprint, or JWK) for a . The field is ALWAYS the SHA-256 hex of the raw secret — never plaintext.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ClientCredential.Value
member.summary: SHA-256 hex of the raw secret. Never store the plaintext value here.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ClientCredential.Type
member.summary: Credential type discriminator. Allowed values: "SharedSecret", "X509Thumbprint", "JsonWebKey".

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ClientCredential.Description
member.summary: Optional human-readable description of this credential.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ClientCredential.Expiration
member.summary: Optional UTC expiry. If set, the credential is rejected after this time.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.ResourceCredential
member.summary: A hashed credential for a (used by introspection callers). The field is ALWAYS SHA-256 hex — never plaintext.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ResourceCredential.Value
member.summary: SHA-256 hex of the raw secret.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ResourceCredential.Type
member.summary: Credential type. Allowed: "SharedSecret".

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ResourceCredential.Description
member.summary: Optional human-readable description.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ResourceCredential.Expiration
member.summary: Optional UTC expiry.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.ClientClaim
member.summary: A static claim that is always included in tokens issued for a .

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ClientClaim.Type
member.summary: Claim type (e.g., "role", "tenant").

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ClientClaim.Value
member.summary: Claim value.

member.name: T:SimpleAuth.ClientJwks
member.summary: A JSON Web Key set (inline JWKS) for a client using private_key_jwt authentication.

member.name: P:SimpleAuth.ClientJwks.Json
member.summary: Raw JSON of the JWKS document. Must contain public keys only.
